I strongly condemn the heinous bomb explosion near Chaman Phatak, Quetta, which has resulted in the tragic loss of innocent lives and left many others injured.

Such cowardly acts of terrorism cannot weaken the resolve of the people of Pakistan. We remain steadfast in our determination to eliminate terrorism in all its forms and manifestations.

I express my heartfelt condolences to the families of the victims and pray for the swift recovery of the injured. The entire nation stands in solidarity with the people of Balochistan in this hour of grief.

tweets.title: Congratulations 🥂
tweets.content:
Congratulations 🥂

Vivek Krishnan (@Viv_Krishnan)

Been 9 years since the @sssdefence Viper was born. It’ll finally go into service with 2 amazing police forces in less than a month.
This is what rebirth looks like. An entire generation of young weapon designers & engineers gave it new essence. I love the new spirit, it’s infectious. This is not India that apologises, explains or asks permission. It’s unabashedly, unapologetically, unmistakably BHARAT.
And we will not change the weapon’s name. It will forever be the Viper. A sticker is not soul. The honesty - knowing what is yours & what is not is wisdom that the Bhagwad Gita teaches. Some missed that chapter. I pray everyday that we have memory of our origin @AlishaMachani @machani_satish

— https://nitter.net/Viv_Krishnan/status/2058545023566983530#m

🚨JUST IN: At least 16 killed in train explosion in Pakistan

At least 16 people were killed and 20 others injured after a passenger train was hit by an explosion in Quetta, officials said.

The blast occurred on railway tracks near the Chaman Phatak area while the train was passing, also damaging nearby vehicles.

🚨 BREAKING: Deadly Train Blast Carrying Pakistan Army Personnel Rocks Quetta, Pakistan

At least 24 people were killed and over 50 injured after a powerful bomb explosion targeted a train in Quetta, Balochistan, causing a derailment and chaos at the railway station.

Emerging details:
- Train reportedly carrying army personnel targeted
- Gunfire heard immediately after the blast
- Railway track and a carriage heavily damaged
- Casualties include both civilians and security personnel

The Balochistan Liberation Army (BLA) has reportedly claimed responsibility for the attack.

tweets.title:
🇮🇳🇺🇸 India and the United States have begun high-level delegation talks in New Delhi, with EAM S. Jaishankar and U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio reaffirming the “Comprehensive Global Strategic Partnership” between the two democracies.

Rubio called India a “strategic ally,” while Jaishankar said the partnership now influences regions far beyond South Asia.

Key focus areas include:
- Defence cooperation
- Strategic technologies
- Trade & investment
- Energy security
- Quad coordination

The talks come ahead of the Quad Foreign Ministers’ Meeting in New Delhi on May 26.

tweets.title:
RT by @OsintTV: "By the time you read this letter, I'll be watching over you from the skies..."

These unforgettable words were written by Captain Vijyant Thapar (Vir Chakra) shortly before he led his assault team to capture the strategic Three Pimples position during the Battle of Tololing in the 1999 Kargil War.

At just 22 years old, Captain Thapar laid down his life for the nation with unmatched courage and honor. Even in his final moments, his thoughts reflected humanity and selflessness — from expressing his wish to donate his organs to ensuring continued support for a young Kashmiri girl, Ruksana, whom he had met during deployment.
His bravery, compassion, and sacrifice continue to inspire generations of Indians.

"Live life king size" — a line that became immortal with him.
tweets.content:
"By the time you read this letter, I'll be watching over you from the skies..."

These unforgettable words were written by Captain Vijyant Thapar (Vir Chakra) shortly before he led his assault team to capture the strategic Three Pimples position during the Battle of Tololing in the 1999 Kargil War.

At just 22 years old, Captain Thapar laid down his life for the nation with unmatched courage and honor. Even in his final moments, his thoughts reflected humanity and selflessness — from expressing his wish to donate his organs to ensuring continued support for a young Kashmiri girl, Ruksana, whom he had met during deployment.
His bravery, compassion, and sacrifice continue to inspire generations of Indians.

"Live life king size" — a line that became immortal with him.

tweets.title:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
23rd May 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● Iran’s Mission to the UN stated that the NPT Review Conference has failed for the third consecutive time, blaming “obstructionism by the US and its allies.”

● An MoU has been reached via Pakistani mediators that notably excludes the key issue of Iran’s enriched uranium stockpile. Both sides are now awaiting a formal US response. Previous US positions have rejected frameworks that sideline the nuclear program.

● GPS and navigation jamming has intensified across the GCC, with significant disruptions reported at Abu Dhabi’s airport.

● UKMTO reported that vessels are being approached by skiffs in the region. One large skiff with two outboard engines was observed carrying ladders and weapons.

● The IRGC Navy’s Public Relations Office announced that 25 vessels transited the Strait of Hormuz in the past 24 hours after obtaining authorization and coordinating with Iranian naval forces.

● CENTCOM reached the milestone of redirecting 100 commercial vessels while enforcing a maritime blockade against Iran.

END OF THE BRIEF
tweets.content:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
23rd May 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● Iran’s Mission to the UN stated that the NPT Review Conference has failed for the third consecutive time, blaming “obstructionism by the US and its allies.”

● An MoU has been reached via Pakistani mediators that notably excludes the key issue of Iran’s enriched uranium stockpile. Both sides are now awaiting a formal US response. Previous US positions have rejected frameworks that sideline the nuclear program.

● GPS and navigation jamming has intensified across the GCC, with significant disruptions reported at Abu Dhabi’s airport.

● UKMTO reported that vessels are being approached by skiffs in the region. One large skiff with two outboard engines was observed carrying ladders and weapons.

● The IRGC Navy’s Public Relations Office announced that 25 vessels transited the Strait of Hormuz in the past 24 hours after obtaining authorization and coordinating with Iranian naval forces.

● CENTCOM reached the milestone of redirecting 100 commercial vessels while enforcing a maritime blockade against Iran.

END OF THE BRIEF

Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
22nd May 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● The Trump administration paused a $14 billion weapons sale to Taiwan to preserve U.S. munitions for the Iran war, according to Acting Navy Secretary Hung Cao.

● The UAE joined Saudi Arabia and Qatar in urging President Trump to prioritize diplomacy with Iran rather than resuming military action.

● Secretary of State Marco Rubio described Iran’s maneuvers in vital trade corridors as a “non-negotiable hurdle” in current diplomatic talks.

● Pakistan’s military leader, General Asim Munir, is en route to Iran as part of ongoing mediation
efforts between Washington and Tehran.

● UKMTO reported that a tanker was approached by a small craft carrying five people 98 nm north of Socotra. The vessel’s armed security team fired warning shots, causing the craft to alter course.

● As of today, CENTCOM has redirected 97 commercial vessels and disabled 4 since the start of the blockade.

END OF BRIEF

- Focus on infiltration, drones, fake currency & hybrid threats
tweets.content:
Union Home Minister Amit Shah says the government will ensure “no unnatural demographic changes” in India by strengthening anti-infiltration measures and deporting illegal infiltrators.

Key announcements:
- “Smart Border” system with drones, radars & surveillance tech
- High-powered demography mission to identify infiltration routes
- Greater coordination between BSF, police & local administration
- Focus on infiltration, drones, fake currency & hybrid threats

The launch validated all operational and technical parameters and was conducted under the Strategic Forces Command.

tweets.title:
R to @NetramDefence: Conclusion: The Net Security Provider

The expansion of India's defense industrial base is no longer just an economic objective; it is a foundational pillar of its foreign policy. By coupling 100% Foreign Direct Investment in specialized defense sectors with an aggressive export strategy, India is positioning itself as a reliable, scalable alternative to traditional defense suppliers. This industrial capacity is what ultimately underwrites India's status as a net security provider in the Indo-Pacific theater.

6/6
tweets.content:
Conclusion: The Net Security Provider

The expansion of India's defense industrial base is no longer just an economic objective; it is a foundational pillar of its foreign policy. By coupling 100% Foreign Direct Investment in specialized defense sectors with an aggressive export strategy, India is positioning itself as a reliable, scalable alternative to traditional defense suppliers. This industrial capacity is what ultimately underwrites India's status as a net security provider in the Indo-Pacific theater.

6/6
tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title:
R to @NetramDefence: Uncrewed Systems and AI Integration

The industrial base is moving beyond legacy platforms to focus on autonomous and uncrewed systems.  

Maritime Domain Awareness: Co-development partnerships, such as those advancing Uncrewed Surface Vehicles (USVs), are critical for persistent ocean monitoring across the vast Indo-Pacific sea lanes.  

Military AI: The defense sector has approved over 120 AI projects across the services, actively transitioning from a compliance-heavy posture to operational deployment. To maintain an edge, the focus remains on deploying AI for sensor fusion, automated threat recognition, and optimizing command and control architectures.

5/6
tweets.content:
Uncrewed Systems and AI Integration

The industrial base is moving beyond legacy platforms to focus on autonomous and uncrewed systems.

Maritime Domain Awareness: Co-development partnerships, such as those advancing Uncrewed Surface Vehicles (USVs), are critical for persistent ocean monitoring across the vast Indo-Pacific sea lanes.

Military AI: The defense sector has approved over 120 AI projects across the services, actively transitioning from a compliance-heavy posture to operational deployment. tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title:
R to @NetramDefence: Advancing Tech Corridors and MRO Infrastructure

Establishing a Regional MRO Hub

Sustaining military readiness in the Indo-Pacific requires highly distributed logistics. Recognizing its advantageous geographic position between Europe and Southeast Asia, India has implemented significant incentives, including a uniform 5% GST rate on aircraft engine parts and extended re-import periods, to establish itself as a regional Maintenance, Repair, and Overhaul (MRO) hub for aerospace and maritime assets.

4/6

tweets.author: @NetramDefence

tweets.title:
R to @NetramDefence: India’s strategy in the Indo-Pacific has evolved from conducting joint maritime exercises to transferring hard, strategic assets. Providing material capability to regional partners is the most direct method of ensuring a free and open Indo-Pacific order.

The Philippines: Delivery of the BrahMos supersonic cruise missile batteries to Manila establishes a credible coastal deterrence architecture.

Vietnam: The gifting of the operational missile corvette INS Kirpan represents a shift toward enabling regional partners with active naval platforms.

Singapore and Indonesia: Ongoing exports of radar distribution cards, amplifiers, and naval gun systems integrate Indian hardware into Southeast Asian defense networks.

Hardware exports serve as instruments of strategic diplomacy. Supplying neighboring nations with precision strike and surveillance capabilities directly checks the unilateral expansion of adversary powers without requiring a massive forward deployment of Indian forces. 

3/6

Hardware exports serve as instruments of strategic diplomacy. Supplying neighboring nations with precision strike and surveillance capabilities directly checks the unilateral expansion of adversary powers without requiring a massive forward deployment of Indian forces.

R to @NetramDefence: The growth in production is no longer exclusively driven by Defense Public Sector Undertakings (DPSUs). The stabilization of India's Defense Industrial Corridors and updated procurement procedures have galvanized the private sector.  
Capitalizing on 

Market Gaps: Indigenous private defense manufacturers have advanced small arms and tactical systems, are actively capturing market share amid fragmented global supply chains.

Venture Integration: Military-tech startup funding has scaled aggressively, with cumulative investments surpassing ₹5,240 crore ($611 million) across over 200 funding rounds.

2/6
